task_completion.description: Zotero application is open and the main library view is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero application window is visible on the screen, showing the library pane.

task_completion.description: The LSTM paper is present in the Zotero library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title 'Long Short-Term Memory' and authors 'Hochreiter' and 'Schmidhuber' are visible in the Zotero library list.

task_completion.description: The Autoencoder paper is present in the Zotero library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title 'Reducing the Dimensionality of Data with Neural Networks' and authors 'Hinton' and 'Salakhutdinov' are visible in the Zotero library list.

task_completion.description: The LeNet paper is present in the Zotero library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title 'Gradient-based learning applied to document recognition' and authors including 'LeCun', 'Bottou', and 'Bengio' are visible in the Zotero library list.

task_completion.description: The added papers display complete metadata.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the library list columns or the right-hand details pane, the imported papers show complete metadata such as publication year, publication title (e.g., Neural Computation, Science, Proceedings of the IEEE), and full author lists.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to add the items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Zotero interface (e.g., the 'Add Item(s) by Identifier' magic wand button or dialog), rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The items are shown inside the actual Zotero application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Zotero application window with its standard layout (collections pane, items list, details pane) is clearly visible, not a text editor or mocked interface.

integrity.description: The metadata appears to be fetched automatically rather than manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The metadata is rich and accurately formatted (e.g., correct casing, full journal names, multiple authors), which is characteristic of automatic DOI fetching rather than manual entry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires adding three specific papers to Zotero using DOIs. 1. DOI 10.1162/neco.1997.9.8.1735 corresponds to 'Long Short-Term Memory' by Hochreiter and Schmidhuber (1997). 2. DOI 10.1126/science.1127647 corresponds to 'Reducing the Dimensionality of Data with Neural Networks' by Hinton and Salakhutdinov (2006). 3. DOI 10.1109/5.726791 corresponds to 'Gradient-based learning applied to document recognition' by LeCun, Bottou, Bengio, and Haffner (1998). The expected outcome is exactly these 3 items added with their complete metadata.
